title: Non-Emergency Medical Transportation - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Non-Emergency Medical Transportation 88 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions AIDS/HIV Breast Cancer Cancer Medicaid Recipients Older Adults Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advanced Health is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Advanced Health Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH BAY CITIES BROKERAGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-emergency transportation. Transportation can be scheduled to and from covered medical appointments and for pharmacy pickups. Transportation services are intended to help when no other options are available. Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Advanced Health Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 266-4323 Visit Website More Details AGE+ CIRCLES OF CARE THE DALLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Matches volunteers with older adults to provide assistance such as friendly visits or phone calls, transportation to medical appointments, grocery shopping, light housework, and yard work. Volunteer opportunities are also available. Eligibility: Supportive services: Age 55 and older Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (503) 408-0724 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH BROOKINGS OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in AllCare Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Nurse Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 471-4106 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH BROOKINGS OFFICE FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offeres funds in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (888) 460-0185 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MAIN OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in AllCare Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Nurse Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (844) 747-5656 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MAIN OFFICE FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offeres funds in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 471-4106 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MEDFORD OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF AllCare CCO coordinates health insurance services for Medicaid members assigned to its Coordinated Care Organization (CCO). Includes specialized information and referrals to healthcare providers for medical, dental, and behavioral health. Additional wellness benefits for AllCare CCO members include medical transportation, pain management, weight management programs, and a 24/7 nurse help line. AllCare CCO members can receive vouchers from providers for taking healthy actions such as attending appointments and tobacco cessation, to purchase items at the BABE Store such as diapers, books, and blankets. Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in AllCare Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Nurse Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 734-5520 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MEDFORD OFFICE FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offeres funds in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (888) 460-0185 Visit Website More Details ANGEL FLIGHT WEST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Arranges air travel through donated flights. Missions flown include non-emergency medical transport to far-off medical treatment, specialty camps, domestic violence relocation, assistance for military personnel, blood/organ/tissue donations, and disaster response. Trips also cover compassion-related reasons, including but not limited to travel to visit or provide care for a critically ill family member. The distance of travel is 101 miles minimum and 899 miles maximum. Requests must be submitted at least 7 days in advance and potential clients must have a physician's medical release to travel. Requests are accepted on a case-by-case basis and assistance is not guaranteed. 3161 Donald Douglas Loop S Santa Monica, CA 90405 Eligibility: Individuals who are medically stable and fully ambulatory with a financial, geographical, or other compelling reason for needing a donated flight Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (310) 390-2958 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAREOREGON COORDINATED HEALTH CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF CareOregon is a Managed Care Organization (MCO) that coordinates Medicaid services for Coordinated Care Organizations (CCO) Jackson Care Connect and Columbia Pacific. CareOregon also provides health insurance services through its partnering CCO, Health Share. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, substance use disorder treatment, and non-emergency transportation for health care visits covered by the CCO. Eligibility: For CCO services: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members in the Portland metro region currently assigned to Health Share-CareOregon CCO Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE HEALTH ALLIANCE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Cascade Health Alliance is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Cascade Health Alliance Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-2947 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PENDLETON CARE-RIDE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-emergency rides to urgent care or the emergency room. 500 SW Dorion Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 5am-10pm (541) 276-8294 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for medical appointments, personal business, and shopping. Priority is given to life-sustaining medical appointments. Rides are provided within Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties. The Ride Together program allows riders to recruit their own volunteer driver (i.e., family, neighbors, and friends). Participants schedule rides with their volunteer driver. Mileage reimbursement is provided, and volunteer training is required. The Vets Driving Vets program matches veterans who volunteer their services with other veterans who need rides to doctors' appointments or to take care of other basic needs. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 65 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Transportation services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 655-8208 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLEARVIEW DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers private and non-emergency medical transportation services. 1114 SW Frazer Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Low-income households, adults with disabilities, or age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 276-1130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Columbia Pacific CCO is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, substance use disorder treatment, mental health care, and non-emergency medical transportation for health care visits covered by the CCO. Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Columbia Pacific CCO Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 488-2822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC BAKER COUNTY VETERAN TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation to medical appointments for veterans. 2810 Cedar Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-4pm Email (541) 523-6591 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC WALLOWA COUNTY VETERAN TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation to medical appointments for veterans. 702 NW First Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-4pm Email (541) 426-3840 1204 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORPORATE ANGEL NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides air transportation for cancer patients, bone marrow donors or recipients, and stem cell donors or recipients on a corporate aircraft. 107 Mill Plain Road Danbury, CT 06811 Eligibility: -Financial need is not a requirement -Cancer patients must be traveling to or from a nationally recognized cancer treatment center -Must be able to walk up the steps of a private jet without assistance, and no need for oxygen, IV, or medical assistance during the flight Hours: Monday-Thursday 6am-2pm, Friday 6am-1pm Email (914) 328-1313 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:00am-5pm Email (541) 322-7155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIAL-A-BUS LINN COUNTY MEDICAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers non-emergency transportation to medical, mental health, and dental appointments for residents traveling from Linn to Benton County. 4077 SW Research Way Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Only provides rides for Benton County medical appointments. Youth age 12 and under must ride with an adult. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm. Rides may be provided outside of these hours. Follows the Corvallis School District inclement weather closure policy and closed on federal holidays. Email (541) 752-2615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DAV TRANSPORTATION NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transportation to and from the Portland VA Medical Center. Does not provide wheelchair-accessible transportation or door-to-door service. 100 SW Main Street Room 212a Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Veterans who have a verifiable appointment at Portland VA Medical Center. Hours: Varies (503) 721-7804 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2102 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 612-4028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING ONTARIO HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1021 SW 5th Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING PENDLETON HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 322 SW 3rd Avenue Pendleton, OR 978012174 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING THE DALLES HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 276-1037 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization (EOCCO) is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportion to medical appointments, shopping, social, or business trips, with medical being the priority. 615 E Cottonwood Lane Condon, OR 97823 Eligibility: Unrestricted; age 60 and older and people with disabilities are prioritized Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Transportation available Monday-Friday, and occasional weekends Email (541) 351-9545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEALTH SHARE OF OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Health Share of Oregon is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Health Share of Oregon; members are able to choose medical care through CareOregon, Kaiser Permanente, Legacy Health, OHSU, or Providence. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:45pm Email (503) 416-8090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides HIV testing, expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1109 June Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 386-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER VALLEY ADULT CENTER CIRCLES OF CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services to assist with daily needs including help with everyday chores, grocery shopping, light housework, yard work, local transportation, and friendly check-ins. Services are provided by volunteers and program accepts new volunteers's. 2010 Sterling Place Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Supportive services: Age 55 and older Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 386-2060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO SENIOR SERVICES CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 10165 SE Cherry Blossom Drive Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTERCOMMUNITY HEALTH NETWORK CCO COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF InterCommunity Health Network is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in InterCommunity Health Network CCO Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 768-7863 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON CARE CONNECT COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Jackson Care Connect is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, substance use disorder treatment, and non-emergency medical transportation for health care visits covered by the CCO. Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Jackson Care Connect Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 500-0567 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential rapid testing for HIV/AIDS, syphilis, and hepatitis C. Offers prevention education and treatment support. Assists with access to HIV medications, transportation to medical appointments, transitional housing, disease management education, and referrals for basic needs, employment, and substance use treatment. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MALHEUR COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) serve as the entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors. CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in health care -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Transportation to medical appointments -Oregon Veterans' Home Conservatorship services, including total estate management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law 316 NE Goodfellow Street Suite 4 Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouses, surviving spouses who have not remarried, children or stepchildren) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Vale Senior Center Outreach (100 Longfellow Street S, Vale, OR 97918): 1st Wednesday of the month 11am-1pm Email (541) 889-6649 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MERCY MEDICAL ANGELS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gas cards, bus tickets, train tickets, and air transportation for patients and medically necessary caregivers traveling to distant medical appointments. 101 W Main Street Suite 1000 Norfolk, VA 23510 Eligibility: Income must be at or below 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Monday-Thursday 4am-2pm Email (757) 318-9174 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORROW COUNTY PUBLIC TRANSIT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides public transit options. Eligibility: Morrow County residents Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Ride hours vary by destination Email (541) 676-5667 541-676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTIPLE SCLEROSIS FOUNDATION MS FOCUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide array of services for those living with multiple sclerosis (MS), including grants for emergency financial assistance, homecare services, assistive technology, laptop computers, cooling accessories, and medical appointment transportation or car repair services. Additionally, connects people to educational events, support groups, and offers access to a lending library, subject to availability. 6520 North Andrews Avenue Fort Lauderdale, FL 33309 Eligibility: Persons afflicted by MS Hours: Helpline: 7 days per week 6am-4pm Website: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 673-6287 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION CENTRAL OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 510 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3646 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION EAST AREA OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 600 NE 8th Street Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3840 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION MID-COUNTY OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 11982 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-5480 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION NORTH/NORTHEAST OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 5325 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-5470 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION SOUTHEAST OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 4610 SE Belmont Street 2nd Floor Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3660 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION WEST AREA OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, and transportation. 209 SW 4th Avenue 1st floor Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-5460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NON-EMERGENCY TRANSPORTATION FOR OHP MEMBERS BAY CITIES BROKERAGE NON-EMERGENCY MEDICAL TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-emergency rides to health care appointments or pharmacy visits. OHP will only pay for travel costs for rides services approved before the appointment or pharmacy visit. Eligibility: OHP members enrolled with [name of CCO] and enrolled in OHP Plus (BMM, BMH, BMD) benefits Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (877) 324-8109 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NON-EMERGENCY TRANSPORTATION FOR OHP MEMBERS CASCADES EAST RIDE CENTER NON-EMERGENCY MEDICAL TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-emergency rides to health care appointments or pharmacy visits. OHP will only pay for travel costs for rides services approved before the appointment or pharmacy visit. Appointments must be made at least 3 days prior to service. 501 NW 25th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 714-0820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY CARE WELLRIDE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF WellRide services are a covered Medicaid benefit for non-emergency medical transportation. Transportation can be scheduled to and from covered medical appointments, including primary care visits, specialist office visits, physical therapy, dental care visits, mental health services, and dialysis. Services include bus fare, pre-approved mileage reimbursement, wheelchair lift/ramp van, taxi cab, stretcher car for those who need to be transported lying down, and secured transport for those in a mental health crisis. If needed, personal care attendants can also ride at no cost. Transportation services are intended to help when no other options are available. Eligibility: OHP members assigned to Yamhill Community Care. Not available for people with CAWM coverage.
